content: agent1: implementing 'Add Token Position Tracking' feature - adding return_positions param to Encoding.encode() in tiktoken/core.py. Will touch tiktoken/core.py only. Let me know if you're also touching this file.

agent1: plan for core.py - adding kwarg 'return_positions: bool = False' to encode() signature (placed after disallowed_special), plus new private helper methods _encode_with_positions/_encode_ordinary_with_positions and a cached _compiled_pat property near the bottom of the class. I'll only touch encode()'s signature/docstring/body minimally (adding an early-return branch when return_positions=True) and add new methods after _encode_bytes. Let me know your exact insertion points for 'transformers' so we don't clobber each other's Edit calls.

agent1: done with my part. I added 'return_positions: bool = False' as the last kwarg in encode()'s signature, one early-return branch (if return_positions: return self._encode_with_positions(...)) placed right after the disallowed_special check and before the try/except _core_bpe.encode call, plus new private helper methods (_compiled_pat, _encode_with_positions, _encode_with_positions_inner, _encode_ordinary_with_positions) inserted after _encode_bytes near the bottom of the class. I did not touch the top of encode()'s body, so your 'transformers' param at the top should merge cleanly. All existing tests pass on my end. Let me know if you see conflicts when you check the file.
